concept.name: Best-effort delivery
concept.definition: The way the internet moves packets with no promises: it tries to deliver each one, but packets can be lost, duplicated, delayed, or arrive out of order.

audience: a curious adult with no domain background
atomic_reason: The mechanism itself fits in one short figure, like mail sent with no tracking where some letters vanish, arrive twice, or show up late and shuffled. But the whole idea is about what happens to packets, and a curious adult with no background does not yet know that data travels as small labeled chunks, so that idea needs its own picture first.

prerequisites.name: Data packets
prerequisites.definition: Small labeled chunks that a message is chopped into before being sent across a network, each traveling on its own and carrying enough information to be put back in order.
prerequisites.why: Best-effort delivery describes the fate of individual packets in transit, so without first picturing a message as separate chunks traveling on their own, there is nothing for the losses, duplicates, delays, and reorderings to happen to.
